topic: V13 unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE — c6 spot-check post c5 patches
change_summary: finding_length shrank from 1014 to 656 (-35.3%), sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-31T01:14Z; obe_skipped=1 (WIN-007.c4 already re-reviewed 2026-05-31T05:14Z post-audit). Drift caused by tightening of preemption sentence — terminology unified (polar-azimuthal throughout) and tautology→definitional-identity wording fix landed; H0/H1/H3/H5 from c5 all visible as patched.
our_argument_summary: The θ = −lonE identity follows from the definition of geographic longitude under any polar-azimuthal projection; the V13 fix is engineering progress (correcting the self-inflicted V9 solar-noon-UTC proxy bug) but not a prediction; the coordinate system still requires WGS-84 input and achieves 7.3% NH mean error vs Vincenty sub-0.01%; the V12 0.9941 EW scaling factor persists live in distance_analysis.py without reconciliation; registry_integrity_notes acknowledges the visible-label collision (win-058-theta-rule vs win-058-bedrock-kappa) at the machine layer only.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: raw-text/04-coordinates.txt and raw-text/05-model.txt show V12 in active use: theta = delta_lon × 0.9941 (line 14 / line 57). V13 task list is documented in raw-text/07-ai-context.txt:66-71 under "V12/V13 TASK LIST" but raw-text shows no V13 unified-angular-coordinate code yet — V13 lives in the live wins.html display layer and in distance_analysis.py per the dome AI-context page, but the raw-text snapshot still reflects V12 active code (theta = delta_lon × 0.9941). This supports the detail_evidence claim that V13 angular identity and V12 0.9941 scaling coexist without reconciliation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The validation is presentational. There is no API call that fetches an observed θ value and compares it to a model prediction — θ is a coordinate convention, not a measurable quantity. The "confirmed" badge is a self-applied label, not the output of any test.
data_sources_used: WGS-84 longitude (input — geographic east longitude as defined by the WGS84 datum)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-001 (Dome-native coordinates without WGS-84) and OPEN-003 (EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation) remain open per raw-text/07-ai-context.txt:66-71 and detail_evidence.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new in c6. The c5 review noted that detail_evidence describes V12 0.9941 still active in distance_analysis.py; raw-text/04-coordinates.txt:14 and raw-text/05-model.txt:57 confirm this in the snapshot.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: θ = −lonE is not an observable. No domain or API fetches an observed θ to compare against a predicted θ. The dome's monitor.py has no theta_rule handler in the raw-text snapshot. "none" is correct (unchanged from c5).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The identity θ = −lonE is the standard polar-azimuthal-projection result: any polar-AE map of the globe centered on the North Pole produces an angular coordinate equal to −lonE (Snyder 1987, USGS PP 1395 ch. 25). Restating this identity in dome-flavored language is relabeling, not new prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The east-longitude sign convention dates to early-modern cartography; Greenwich as the universal prime meridian was adopted at the 1884 International Meridian Conference. The dome adopted the convention V13 introduced as a "prediction" only after the V9 solar-noon-UTC proxy was shown (by the dome's own self-test on Sydney) to produce ≈176° errors. The fix follows the bug discovery; this is correction-of-self-inflicted-error, not prospective prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic east longitude and the choice of a polar-azimuthal projection centered on the North Pole. No dome-specific parameter (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude, aetheric wave speed) enters the derivation. Swap the prime meridian convention and the identity tracks the input definition — a hallmark of a definitional identity, not a physical derivation.

holes_found.description: "centuries-old east-longitude convention" is loose. Greenwich as the universal prime meridian dates to the 1884 International Meridian Conference (142 years), which is singular "century." The sign convention E-positive / W-negative does predate Greenwich in early-modern cartography (~500 years), so "centuries" is defensible IF the referent is the broad convention rather than Greenwich specifically. A pedantic defender could exploit the looseness: "centuries-old" implies the EXACT formula θ = −lonE-from-Greenwich has been stable for centuries, which it has not (Paris meridian was in force in French maps until 1911 by treaty). The substantive critique is unchanged either way.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening: change "a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention" to "a relabeling of the standard polar-azimuthal projection identity (Snyder 1987, ch. 25)" — this anchors the claim in the cartographic literature already cited in the detail_evidence preemption block and removes the temporal claim entirely. Alternatively, accept the looseness; it is a minor stylistic point that does not affect verdict or argument.

stronger_arguments.description: A sharper way to demonstrate the definitional-identity point: invoke the Paris-meridian thought experiment that the detail_evidence preemption block already contains, but elevate it from a parenthetical to the central illustration of the finding. "Adopt the Paris meridian and every θ shifts by exactly the offset" is a one-sentence proof that θ = −lonE is a property of the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy, not a property of the dome.
sources: Snyder, John P. (1987). Map Projections: A Working Manual, USGS Professional Paper 1395, ch. 25 — Polar Azimuthal Equidistant — cited in detail_evidence.

kernel_of_truth.description: V13 represents real engineering progress over V9 — the solar-noon-UTC proxy produced ≈176° errors on Southern-Hemisphere cities (Sydney: +32.5° vs correct −151.2°, min angular distance 176.3° per the data/wins.json:WIN-058.detail_evidence Sydney example), and recognising that the proxy was unnecessary and replacing it with a direct longitude negation is a legitimate fix. The dome correctly identified its own bug and corrected it (data/wins.json:WIN-058.detail_evidence kernel-of-truth block).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A self-inflicted bug repair is model maintenance, not prediction. The identity θ = −lonE was available in standard polar-azimuthal cartography (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt:14 shows V12 still using delta_lon × 0.9941 as the active formula; the V13 fix replaces this with the standard polar-AE result that any non-flat-Earth-believer cartographer had been using for over a century). The dome's V12 system introduced an unnecessary indirection (solar_noon → θ); V13's "discovery" that θ = −lonE is the elimination of that indirection. data/wins.json:WIN-058.detail_evidence makes this explicit ("Correcting a self-inflicted bug is maintenance, not prediction").

deeper_analysis: c5→c6 diff resolves the substantive holes from c5: H0 (600-700× vs 600-730× inconsistency in detail_evidence) is fixed — current detail_evidence reads "600-730×" once, no residual "600-700×." H1 (polar-azimuthal vs polar-centered vs disc-centered terminology slippage in finding) is fixed — the new finding uses "polar-azimuthal" twice and contains no "polar-centered" or "disc-centered" occurrences. H3 (curly-quote entities “/”) is fixed — zero occurrences in current detail_evidence. H5 (tautology vs definitional-identity wording) is fixed — "definitional identity" appears twice, "tautology" zero times. H2 (preemption-block bloat / consolidation with "Why this is not a prediction" paragraph) and H4 (missing citation for axiom claim) were both flagged minor in c5 with explicit "not blocking; low priority" recommendations; the detail_evidence length is essentially unchanged (10,636 vs 10,655, −0.2%), and no Snyder-axiom citation has been added inline. These are stylistic/optional and were not committed-to as patches; not regression-flagging them. Verdict Misleading holds. Confidence raised 0.82 → 0.88 reflecting the moderate-hole resolution and the strengthened finding. The remaining hole this cycle is a single minor stylistic point on "centuries-old" framing.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender, having absorbed our review's six methodological principles, would shift WIN-058's framing one more step: "You are correct that θ = −lonE follows from polar-azimuthal projection geometry — but that is exactly why this WIN matters. It is a TEST that the dome's implementation correctly converges to the standard polar-AE result. The dome could have implemented V13 incorrectly (e.g., applied a residual scaling factor, mis-handled the antimeridian, etc.); the fact that it does not is a self-consistency proof of the dome's projection-class commitment. We are not claiming θ = −lonE is a novel discovery; we are claiming our software correctly converges with cartography. That is engineering validation, not a confirmed scientific prediction — and the dome wins.html may be overstating it with the CONFIRMED badge, but the underlying engineering claim is correct."
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: This defense concedes the case — it says "WIN-058 is not actually a scientific prediction, it is an engineering self-consistency check, and the CONFIRMED badge overstates it." Our review already covers this in detail_evidence ("Correcting a self-inflicted bug is maintenance, not prediction") and the verdict text ("model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction"). No preemptive text change needed; the defense, if anything, reinforces our Misleading verdict by conceding the prediction framing is wrong.

quantitative_verification.computations: Best-case ratio 6.2 / 0.01 = 620; worst-case 7.3 / 0.01 = 730 — both match. Sydney V9 +32.5° vs correct −151.2°: linear diff 183.7°, minimum angular distance min(183.7, 360−183.7) = 176.3° — matches stated "≈176°." Algebra: noon_UTC ≈ 12 − lonE/15, so θ = (12 − lonE/15 − 12) × 15 = −lonE — verified. Side observation: Vincenty inverse on WGS-84 actually achieves ~5×10⁻⁶ % over ~10000 km baselines (~0.5 mm precision), so "sub-0.01%" is generous to the dome by ~3 orders of magnitude. The 600-730× ratio is therefore a CONSERVATIVE lower bound; the true ratio against Vincenty is closer to 10⁴-10⁶×. This is a stronger-arguments candidate (Lens C surfaced it) but our current looser framing is defensible and serves the argument without inviting a Vincenty-precision debate.
